```{r}
# =======================
# PACKAGES
# =======================
library(readr)
library(dplyr)
library(stringr)
library(ggplot2)
library(pracma)
library(lme4)
library(lmerTest)
library(emmeans)
library(purrr)
library(broom)
```

```{r}
# =======================
# INPUT / OUTPUT
# =======================
file <- "input_files/timing_of_Draxin_addition.csv"
out_dir <- "output/growth_assays/timing"
dir.create(out_dir, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)

# =======================
# READ DATA
# Required columns:
#   Experiment
#   Replicate
#   Draxin   (used here as treatment/timing condition)
#   Hr  (or Hour)
#   Count
# =======================
df_raw <- read_csv(file, show_col_types = FALSE)

# Allow either Hr or Hour in input
if ("Hr" %in% names(df_raw) && !("Hour" %in% names(df_raw))) {
  df_raw <- df_raw %>% rename(Hour = Hr)
}

required_cols <- c("Experiment", "Replicate", "Draxin", "Hour", "Count")
missing_cols <- setdiff(required_cols, names(df_raw))

if (length(missing_cols) > 0) {
  stop(paste("Missing required columns:", paste(missing_cols, collapse = ", ")))
}

# Rename Draxin column internally to Treatment for clarity
df <- df_raw %>%
  rename(Treatment = Draxin) %>%
  mutate(
    Experiment = factor(Experiment),
    Replicate  = factor(Replicate),
    Treatment  = str_trim(as.character(Treatment)),
    Hour       = as.numeric(Hour),
    Count      = as.numeric(Count)
  ) %>%
  filter(
    !is.na(Experiment),
    !is.na(Replicate),
    !is.na(Treatment),
    !is.na(Hour),
    !is.na(Count)
  )

# Optional manual order
treatment_order <- c("ctrl", "all_the_time", "after_24hr", "only_24hr")
treatment_order_present <- treatment_order[treatment_order %in% unique(df$Treatment)]

if (length(treatment_order_present) == 0) {
  treatment_order_present <- sort(unique(df$Treatment))
}

df <- df %>%
  mutate(Treatment = factor(Treatment, levels = treatment_order_present))

if (!("ctrl" %in% levels(df$Treatment))) {
  stop("Control level 'ctrl' was not found in Treatment column.")
}
```

```{r}
# =======================
# INPUT SANITY CHECKS
# =======================

# 1) Save column names
colnames_df <- data.frame(
  column_name = names(df)
)

write.csv(
  colnames_df,
  file.path(out_dir, "input_column_names.csv"),
  row.names = FALSE
)

# 2) Number of unique values in each column
column_uniques <- data.frame(
  column_name = names(df),
  n_unique = sapply(df, function(x) dplyr::n_distinct(x))
)

write.csv(
  column_uniques,
  file.path(out_dir, "input_n_unique_per_column.csv"),
  row.names = FALSE
)

# 3) Unique values for key columns
unique_experiment <- data.frame(Experiment = sort(unique(as.character(df$Experiment))))
unique_replicate  <- data.frame(Replicate = sort(unique(as.character(df$Replicate))))
unique_treatment  <- data.frame(Treatment = levels(df$Treatment))
unique_hour       <- data.frame(Hour = sort(unique(df$Hour)))

write.csv(
  unique_experiment,
  file.path(out_dir, "unique_Experiment_values.csv"),
  row.names = FALSE
)

write.csv(
  unique_replicate,
  file.path(out_dir, "unique_Replicate_values.csv"),
  row.names = FALSE
)

write.csv(
  unique_treatment,
  file.path(out_dir, "unique_Treatment_values.csv"),
  row.names = FALSE
)

write.csv(
  unique_hour,
  file.path(out_dir, "unique_Hour_values.csv"),
  row.names = FALSE
)

# 4) Number of rows per Experiment × Replicate × Treatment
curve_sizes <- df %>%
  count(Experiment, Replicate, Treatment, name = "n_rows") %>%
  arrange(Experiment, Replicate, Treatment)

write.csv(
  curve_sizes,
  file.path(out_dir, "rows_per_curve.csv"),
  row.names = FALSE
)

# 5) Basic Count summary per curve
curve_count_summary <- df %>%
  group_by(Experiment, Replicate, Treatment) %>%
  summarise(
    n_timepoints = n(),
    min_hour = min(Hour, na.rm = TRUE),
    max_hour = max(Hour, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_count = mean(Count, na.rm = TRUE),
    median_count = median(Count, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_count = sd(Count, na.rm = TRUE),
    min_count = min(Count, na.rm = TRUE),
    max_count = max(Count, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(Experiment, Replicate, Treatment)

write.csv(
  curve_count_summary,
  file.path(out_dir, "count_summary_per_curve.csv"),
  row.names = FALSE
)

# 6) Timepoint coverage per curve as a compact string
timepoint_coverage <- df %>%
  group_by(Experiment, Replicate, Treatment) %>%
  summarise(
    timepoints = paste(sort(unique(Hour)), collapse = ", "),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(Experiment, Replicate, Treatment)

write.csv(
  timepoint_coverage,
  file.path(out_dir, "timepoint_coverage_per_curve.csv"),
  row.names = FALSE
)

# 7) Quick overview in console
cat("\n=======================\n")
cat("INPUT DATA SUMMARY\n")
cat("=======================\n")
cat("Rows:", nrow(df), "\n")
cat("Columns:", ncol(df), "\n\n")

cat("Experiments:", paste(sort(unique(as.character(df$Experiment))), collapse = ", "), "\n")
cat("Replicates:", paste(sort(unique(as.character(df$Replicate))), collapse = ", "), "\n")
cat("Treatments:", paste(levels(df$Treatment), collapse = ", "), "\n")
cat("Hours:", paste(sort(unique(df$Hour)), collapse = ", "), "\n\n")

cat("Rows per Treatment:\n")
print(df %>% count(Treatment))

cat("\nRows per Experiment x Treatment:\n")
print(df %>% count(Experiment, Treatment))

cat("\nDone with input sanity checks.\n\n")
```

```{r}
# =======================
# BASIC QC
# =======================
dup_check <- df %>%
  count(Experiment, Replicate, Treatment, Hour) %>%
  filter(n > 1)

write.csv(
  dup_check,
  file.path(out_dir, "duplicate_timepoints_check.csv"),
  row.names = FALSE
)

baseline_check <- df %>%
  group_by(Experiment, Replicate, Treatment) %>%
  arrange(Hour, .by_group = TRUE) %>%
  summarise(
    n_timepoints = n(),
    baseline_hr = first(Hour),
    baseline_count = first(Count),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

write.csv(
  baseline_check,
  file.path(out_dir, "baseline_check.csv"),
  row.names = FALSE
)

bad_baseline <- baseline_check %>%
  filter(is.na(baseline_count) | baseline_count <= 0)

write.csv(
  bad_baseline,
  file.path(out_dir, "bad_baseline_curves.csv"),
  row.names = FALSE
)
```

```{r}
# =======================
# NORMALIZE TO BASELINE
# within each Experiment × Replicate × Treatment
# =======================
df_norm <- df %>%
  group_by(Experiment, Replicate, Treatment) %>%
  arrange(Hour, .by_group = TRUE) %>%
  mutate(
    baseline_hr = first(Hour),
    baseline_count = first(Count),
    Count_norm = Count / baseline_count
  ) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  filter(
    !is.na(Count_norm),
    is.finite(Count_norm),
    !is.na(baseline_count),
    baseline_count > 0
  )

write.csv(
  df_norm,
  file.path(out_dir, "normalized_timecourse.csv"),
  row.names = FALSE
)
```

```{r}
# =======================
# SUMMARY FOR PLOTTING
# =======================
df_plot <- df_norm %>%
  group_by(Treatment, Hour) %>%
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    mean_norm = mean(Count_norm, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_norm = sd(Count_norm, na.rm = TRUE),
    sem_norm = sd_norm / sqrt(n),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

write.csv(
  df_plot,
  file.path(out_dir, "normalized_timecourse_summary.csv"),
  row.names = FALSE
)

# =======================
# PLOT: normalized growth curves
# =======================
p_norm <- ggplot(df_plot, aes(x = Hour, y = mean_norm, color = Treatment, group = Treatment)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  geom_errorbar(
    aes(ymin = mean_norm - sem_norm, ymax = mean_norm + sem_norm),
    width = 2
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Normalized growth curves",
    x = "Time (h)",
    y = "Cell count normalized to baseline",
    color = "Treatment"
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 13)

ggsave(
  filename = file.path(out_dir, "normalized_growth_curves_mean_sem.png"),
  plot = p_norm,
  width = 8,
  height = 5,
  dpi = 300
)

ggsave(
  filename = file.path(out_dir, "normalized_growth_curves_mean_sem.pdf"),
  plot = p_norm,
  width = 8,
  height = 5
)
```

```{r}
# =======================
# CALCULATE AUC
# one AUC per replicate curve
# =======================
df_auc <- df_norm %>%
  arrange(Experiment, Replicate, Treatment, Hour) %>%
  group_by(Experiment, Replicate, Treatment) %>%
  summarise(
    baseline_hr = first(baseline_hr),
    n_timepoints = n(),
    AUC_norm = if (n() >= 2) trapz(Hour, Count_norm) else NA_real_,
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(AUC_norm))

write.csv(
  df_auc,
  file.path(out_dir, "auc_normalized_by_replicate.csv"),
  row.names = FALSE
)

# =======================
# CALCULATE TREND (SLOPE)
# =======================
df_slope <- df_norm %>%
  group_by(Experiment, Replicate, Treatment) %>%
  arrange(Hour, .by_group = TRUE) %>%
  summarise(
    n_timepoints = n(),
    slope = if (n() >= 2) coef(lm(Count_norm ~ Hour))[2] else NA_real_,
    intercept = if (n() >= 2) coef(lm(Count_norm ~ Hour))[1] else NA_real_,
    r_squared = if (n() >= 2) summary(lm(Count_norm ~ Hour))$r.squared else NA_real_,
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(slope))

write.csv(
  df_slope,
  file.path(out_dir, "trend_slope_by_replicate.csv"),
  row.names = FALSE
)
```

```{r}
# =======================
# MIXED MODEL: AUC
# =======================
if (nrow(df_auc) >= 3 && dplyr::n_distinct(df_auc$Treatment) >= 2) {
  
  m_auc <- lmer(AUC_norm ~ Treatment + (1 | Experiment), data = df_auc)
  anova_auc <- anova(m_auc)
  emm_auc <- emmeans(m_auc, ~ Treatment)
  
  posthoc_auc <- contrast(
    emm_auc,
    method = "trt.vs.ctrl",
    ref = which(levels(df_auc$Treatment) == "ctrl"),
    adjust = "dunnett"
  )
  
  write.csv(
    as.data.frame(anova_auc),
    file.path(out_dir, "auc_mixed_model_anova.csv"),
    row.names = TRUE
  )
  
  write.csv(
    as.data.frame(emm_auc),
    file.path(out_dir, "auc_emmeans.csv"),
    row.names = FALSE
  )
  
  write.csv(
    as.data.frame(posthoc_auc),
    file.path(out_dir, "auc_treatment_vs_ctrl_dunnett.csv"),
    row.names = FALSE
  )
  
} else {
  message("Skipping AUC mixed model: not enough data.")
}
```

```{r}
# =======================
# MIXED MODEL: TREND / SLOPE
# =======================
if (nrow(df_slope) >= 3 && dplyr::n_distinct(df_slope$Treatment) >= 2) {
  
  m_slope <- lmer(slope ~ Treatment + (1 | Experiment), data = df_slope)
  anova_slope <- anova(m_slope)
  emm_slope <- emmeans(m_slope, ~ Treatment)
  
  posthoc_slope <- contrast(
    emm_slope,
    method = "trt.vs.ctrl",
    ref = which(levels(df_slope$Treatment) == "ctrl"),
    adjust = "dunnett"
  )
  
  write.csv(
    as.data.frame(anova_slope),
    file.path(out_dir, "slope_mixed_model_anova.csv"),
    row.names = TRUE
  )
  
  write.csv(
    as.data.frame(emm_slope),
    file.path(out_dir, "slope_emmeans.csv"),
    row.names = FALSE
  )
  
  write.csv(
    as.data.frame(posthoc_slope),
    file.path(out_dir, "slope_treatment_vs_ctrl_dunnett.csv"),
    row.names = FALSE
  )
  
} else {
  message("Skipping slope mixed model: not enough data.")
}
```

```{r}
# =======================
# FULL TIMECOURSE MODEL
# =======================
if (nrow(df_norm) >= 4 && dplyr::n_distinct(df_norm$Treatment) >= 2) {
  
  m_time <- lmer(
    Count_norm ~ Hour * Treatment + (1 | Experiment) + (1 | Experiment:Replicate),
    data = df_norm
  )
  
  anova_time <- anova(m_time)
  
  write.csv(
    as.data.frame(anova_time),
    file.path(out_dir, "timecourse_mixed_model_anova.csv"),
    row.names = TRUE
  )
  
  emtr <- emtrends(m_time, ~ Treatment, var = "Hour")
  posthoc_trend <- contrast(
    emtr,
    method = "trt.vs.ctrl",
    ref = which(levels(df_norm$Treatment) == "ctrl"),
    adjust = "dunnett"
  )
  
  write.csv(
    as.data.frame(emtr),
    file.path(out_dir, "timecourse_emtrends.csv"),
    row.names = FALSE
  )
  
  write.csv(
    as.data.frame(posthoc_trend),
    file.path(out_dir, "timecourse_trend_vs_ctrl_dunnett.csv"),
    row.names = FALSE
  )
  
} else {
  message("Skipping full timecourse mixed model: not enough data.")
}
```

```{r}
# =======================
# TIMEPOINT-SPECIFIC STATS
# treatment vs ctrl at each hour
# Hour treated as factor
# =======================
df_norm_tp <- df_norm %>%
  mutate(Hour_f = factor(Hour, levels = sort(unique(Hour))))

if (nrow(df_norm_tp) >= 4 && dplyr::n_distinct(df_norm_tp$Treatment) >= 2) {
  
  m_time_tp <- lmer(
    Count_norm ~ Treatment * Hour_f + (1 | Experiment) + (1 | Experiment:Replicate),
    data = df_norm_tp
  )
  
  emm_time_by_hour <- emmeans(m_time_tp, ~ Treatment | Hour_f)
  
  contrast_time_vs_ctrl <- contrast(
    emm_time_by_hour,
    method = "trt.vs.ctrl",
    ref = which(levels(df_norm_tp$Treatment) == "ctrl"),
    adjust = "BH"
  )
  
  results_time <- as.data.frame(contrast_time_vs_ctrl) %>%
    mutate(Hour = as.numeric(as.character(Hour_f)))
  
  write.csv(
    results_time,
    file.path(out_dir, "timepoint_specific_vs_ctrl_BH.csv"),
    row.names = FALSE
  )
  
} else {
  message("Skipping timepoint-specific stats: not enough data.")
}
```

```{r}
# =======================
# FIRST SIGNIFICANT TIMEPOINT PER TREATMENT
# =======================
if (exists("results_time") && nrow(results_time) > 0) {
  
  first_sig_time <- results_time %>%
    mutate(
      contrast = as.character(contrast),
      Hour = as.numeric(Hour),
      p.value = as.numeric(p.value)
    ) %>%
    mutate(
      Treatment_test = str_trim(str_remove(contrast, "\\s*-\\s*ctrl$"))
    ) %>%
    group_by(Treatment_test) %>%
    arrange(Hour, .by_group = TRUE) %>%
    summarise(
      first_sig_hour = if (any(p.value < 0.05, na.rm = TRUE)) {
        min(Hour[p.value < 0.05], na.rm = TRUE)
      } else {
        NA_real_
      },
      first_sig_p = if (any(p.value < 0.05, na.rm = TRUE)) {
        p.value[match(first_sig_hour, Hour)]
      } else {
        NA_real_
      },
      .groups = "drop"
    )
  
  write.csv(
    first_sig_time,
    file.path(out_dir, "first_significant_timepoint_per_treatment.csv"),
    row.names = FALSE
  )
  
} else {
  message("No timepoint-specific contrast results found for first-significant-time extraction.")
}
```

```{r}
# =======================
# ENDPOINT DATASET
# last available timepoint per curve
# =======================
df_endpoint <- df_norm %>%
  group_by(Experiment, Replicate, Treatment) %>%
  filter(Hour == max(Hour)) %>%
  ungroup()

write.csv(
  df_endpoint,
  file.path(out_dir, "endpoint_last_timepoint_per_curve.csv"),
  row.names = FALSE
)
```

```{r}
# =======================
# 24 h RAW VALUES
# normalized to ctrl mean within each experiment
# =======================
df_24 <- df %>%
  filter(Hour == 24)

ctrl_24_check <- df_24 %>%
  filter(Treatment == "ctrl") %>%
  group_by(Experiment) %>%
  summarise(
    n_ctrl = n(),
    ctrl_mean_24 = mean(Count, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

write.csv(
  ctrl_24_check,
  file.path(out_dir, "ctrl_24h_check.csv"),
  row.names = FALSE
)

missing_ctrl_experiments <- setdiff(unique(df_24$Experiment), unique(ctrl_24_check$Experiment))
if (length(missing_ctrl_experiments) > 0) {
  warning(
    paste(
      "Some experiments have no 24 h ctrl and will produce NA normalization:",
      paste(missing_ctrl_experiments, collapse = ", ")
    )
  )
}

df_24_norm_ctrl <- df_24 %>%
  group_by(Experiment) %>%
  mutate(
    ctrl_mean_24 = mean(Count[Treatment == "ctrl"], na.rm = TRUE),
    Count_24_norm_to_ctrl = Count / ctrl_mean_24
  ) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  filter(
    !is.na(ctrl_mean_24),
    is.finite(ctrl_mean_24),
    ctrl_mean_24 > 0,
    is.finite(Count_24_norm_to_ctrl)
  )

write.csv(
  df_24_norm_ctrl,
  file.path(out_dir, "raw_24h_normalized_to_ctrl_by_experiment.csv"),
  row.names = FALSE
)

df_24_plot <- df_24_norm_ctrl %>%
  group_by(Treatment) %>%
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    mean_norm = mean(Count_24_norm_to_ctrl, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_norm = sd(Count_24_norm_to_ctrl, na.rm = TRUE),
    sem_norm = sd_norm / sqrt(n),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

write.csv(
  df_24_plot,
  file.path(out_dir, "raw_24h_normalized_to_ctrl_summary.csv"),
  row.names = FALSE
)

p_24 <- ggplot(df_24_norm_ctrl, aes(x = Treatment, y = Count_24_norm_to_ctrl)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 1, linetype = "dashed") +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.12, height = 0, size = 2, alpha = 0.75) +
  geom_point(
    data = df_24_plot,
    aes(x = Treatment, y = mean_norm),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    size = 3
  ) +
  geom_errorbar(
    data = df_24_plot,
    aes(x = Treatment, ymin = mean_norm - sem_norm, ymax = mean_norm + sem_norm),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    width = 0.2
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "24 h raw cell count normalized to experiment-matched ctrl",
    x = "Treatment",
    y = "Cell count at 24 h / ctrl at 24 h"
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 13)

ggsave(
  file.path(out_dir, "raw_24h_normalized_to_ctrl.png"),
  p_24,
  width = 8,
  height = 5,
  dpi = 300
)

ggsave(
  file.path(out_dir, "raw_24h_normalized_to_ctrl.pdf"),
  p_24,
  width = 8,
  height = 5
)
```

```{r}
# =======================
# MIXED MODEL: 24 h DATA
# =======================
if (nrow(df_24_norm_ctrl) >= 3 && dplyr::n_distinct(df_24_norm_ctrl$Treatment) >= 2) {
  
  m_24 <- lmer(
    Count_24_norm_to_ctrl ~ Treatment + (1 | Experiment),
    data = df_24_norm_ctrl
  )
  
  anova_24 <- anova(m_24)
  emm_24 <- emmeans(m_24, ~ Treatment)
  
  posthoc_24 <- contrast(
    emm_24,
    method = "trt.vs.ctrl",
    ref = which(levels(df_24_norm_ctrl$Treatment) == "ctrl"),
    adjust = "dunnett"
  )
  
  write.csv(
    as.data.frame(anova_24),
    file.path(out_dir, "24h_mixed_model_anova.csv"),
    row.names = TRUE
  )
  
  write.csv(
    as.data.frame(emm_24),
    file.path(out_dir, "24h_emmeans.csv"),
    row.names = FALSE
  )
  
  write.csv(
    as.data.frame(posthoc_24),
    file.path(out_dir, "24h_vs_ctrl_dunnett.csv"),
    row.names = FALSE
  )
  
} else {
  message("Skipping 24 h mixed model: not enough data.")
}

cat("Done.\n")
```
